message:
security: Prevent terminal escape sequence injection in OSC 8 and set_title

Adds sanitization for user-controlled strings in terminal escape sequences
to prevent escape sequence injection attacks.

**Threat model:**
An attacker who controls URLs (e.g., via file paths, user input) or window
titles could embed control characters like ESC (0x1B) or BEL (0x07) to:
- Terminate OSC sequences early
- Inject arbitrary terminal commands (clear screen, cursor movement, etc.)
- Potentially exploit terminal emulator vulnerabilities

**OSC 8 Hyperlink protection (src/ansi/mod.rs):**
- New `escape_url_for_osc8()` function percent-encodes C0 control chars
  (0x00-0x1F) and DEL (0x7F) in URLs
- Applied automatically in `write_hyperlink_start()`
- All safe ASCII passes through unchanged for URL readability
- Added comprehensive tests including injection attempt scenarios

**Window title protection (src/terminal/mod.rs):**
- `set_title()` now filters control characters (0x00-0x1F, 0x7F)
- Characters are silently dropped rather than encoded (titles don't need
  control chars for legitimate use)
- Unicode characters (emoji, CJK, etc.) are preserved
- Added tests verifying sanitization and unicode preservation

**Why filter vs encode for titles:**
- URLs have percent-encoding conventions; titles don't
- Filtered titles remain human-readable
- No legitimate use case for control chars in window titles

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
